nodes.title: Medicaid Expansion Program

body.processed:
Columbia's Medicaid Expansion Program provides comprehensive health coverage to eligible low-income adults. Since its launch, the program has enrolled over 350,000 residents who previously lacked health insurance.

Benefits

  • Doctor visits and preventive care
  • Hospital services
  • Prescription medications
  • Mental health and substance abuse treatment
  • Dental and vision coverage

How to Apply

Applications can be submitted online through the Columbia Health Marketplace, by phone, or in person at any county HHS office.
body.summary: Columbia's Medicaid Expansion Program provides comprehensive health coverage to eligible low-income adults. Since its launch, the program has enrolled over 350,000 residents who previously lacked heal

nodes.eligibility: Adults ages 19-64 with income up to 138% of the Federal Poverty Level

nodes.title: Universal Pre-K Initiative

body.processed:
The Universal Pre-K Initiative provides free, high-quality pre-kindergarten education to all four-year-olds in Columbia. The program emphasizes school readiness, social-emotional development, and early literacy skills.

Program Features

  • Full-day pre-kindergarten classes
  • Certified early childhood educators
  • Evidence-based curriculum
  • Family engagement programs

Enrollment

Enrollment opens each spring for the following school year. Families can apply through their local school district or participating community providers.
body.summary: The Universal Pre-K Initiative provides free, high-quality pre-kindergarten education to all four-year-olds in Columbia. The program emphasizes school readiness, social-emotional development, and earl

nodes.eligibility: All four-year-old children residing in Columbia

body.processed:
The Statewide Road Improvement Program is a multi-year initiative to repair, rebuild, and modernize Columbia's road infrastructure. The program addresses structurally deficient bridges, deteriorating highways, and expanding capacity in high-growth corridors.

Program Goals

  • Repair 500 miles of deteriorated highways
  • Replace or rehabilitate 200 structurally deficient bridges
  • Improve traffic flow in 50 congested corridors
  • Expand bicycle and pedestrian infrastructure

body.processed:
The Columbia Clean Energy Initiative aims to transition the state to 80% renewable energy by 2035. The program provides incentives for solar and wind development, energy efficiency improvements, and electric vehicle adoption.

Key Components

  • Solar and wind energy tax credits
  • Home energy efficiency rebates
  • Electric vehicle rebates and charging infrastructure
  • Clean energy workforce training

Impact

Since launch, the initiative has created over 15,000 clean energy jobs and reduced carbon emissions by 18%.
